---
title: "For Department Review"
subtitle: "Cover note for the UBCO Freshwater Science planning resource"
date: last-modified
toc: true
---

# Purpose of this note

This site is a curated advising and planning resource for the
**Bachelor of Science Freshwater Science** major. It exists alongside
,  **not in place of**, the
[UBC Okanagan Academic Calendar](https://okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/),
which remains the authoritative source for all degree requirements.

The site is intended for **two audiences**: current Freshwater Science
students (the headline use case is the in-development interactive
planner) and prospective students browsing the program.

I am sharing this with the **Freshwater Science Program Committee**
and the **Department of Earth, Environmental and Geographic Sciences**
to invite review of three specific judgement calls embedded in the
material. Each is summarised below, with the relevant evidence and the
section of the site where the curated content lives.

# What I want your input on

## 1. The v2 Program Learning Outcomes

The site renders the **v2 PLO list (eight outcomes in four categories)**
finalised in the [28 April 2026 memo](docs/reference/learning-outcomes/UBCO_Memo_PLOs_v2_20260428.docx),
which consolidated the v1 draft of 14 outcomes following:

- a student focus group (Freshwater Science majors),
- a faculty working session, and
- best-practice benchmarking (Penn State, WSU, CSUSB, SUNY, U Michigan-Dearborn,
  plus comparator BSc freshwater programs at UW-Milwaukee, Western
  Michigan, and UW-Madison).

The v1→v2 crosswalk and benchmarking evidence are in §4 and §6 of the
memo, and the eight outcomes themselves are rendered verbatim on the
[Outcomes page](../outcomes/index.qmd).

**Asks**:

- Is the eight-outcome v2 list the final version the Program
  Committee endorses for institutional submission, or should we
  iterate?
- The curriculum-map matrix (per-course I/R/M coding for each PLO)
  is the **next deliverable** from the memo. The
  [EESC 301 pilot mapping](../docs/reference/learning-outcomes/EESC%20301%20-%20Mapping%20to%20EECB%20Program%20Learning%20Outcomes%20-%20NJ.xlsx)
  shows the methodology. We need a path to interview each
  Freshwater Science instructor (≈ 20-40 hours total). Can the
  department support this in fall 2026?

## 2. The RPBio systematics-taxonomy gap

UBCO **does not currently offer** a dedicated undergraduate course in
systematics or taxonomy. This is a real gap for Freshwater Science
students targeting **Registered Professional Biologist (RPBio)**
registration with the College of Applied Biology, because the
[June 2025 CAB Credentialing Standard](https://cab-bc.org/wp-content/uploads/Credentialing_Standard_June-2025.pdf)
lists systematics as one of the five subject areas from which RPBio
candidates must hold at least three. The
[RPBio designation page](../designations/rpbio.qmd) carries a
prominent callout-warning surfacing this gap and recommending that
students either transfer in a systematics course from another
institution or cover the three-of-five via evolution + cell biology +
genetics instead.

**Asks**:

- Should the department **propose a UBCO course** to close this gap
  (e.g., a 300-level "Biodiversity and Systematics" course shared
  with BIOL or EESC)? If yes, this advising site can become the
  forcing function for that proposal.
- In the interim, is the **callout-warning on the RPBio page** an
  acceptable disclosure for students considering this designation,
  or do we want to soften / strengthen the language?

## 3. The designation pathway recommendations

The site surfaces curated 4-year sample plans for **two** professional
designations:

- **Professional Agrologist (PAg)**, BC Institute of Agrologists
- **Registered Professional Biologist (RPBio)**, College of Applied
  Biology

It deliberately **does not** surface pathways for:

- **Professional Geoscientist (P.Geo)**, EGBC. The
  Environmental Geoscience track is technically viable but
  cross-loaded with compulsory geoscience courses (mineralogy,
  sed/strat, structural, geophysics) that compete with the freshwater
  core. Decision: better signposted as a complementary pathway, not a
  headline track.
- **Professional Engineer (P.Eng)**, EGBC. A B.Sc. degree does not
  normally meet CEAB engineering academic standards; this is a
  second-degree path.

Both decisions were made by the program lead based on the
[advising-pack source document](../docs/reference/advising/UBCO_Freshwater_Professional_Paths_Advising_Pack.pdf)
and on what we hear from students. The
[Complementary pathways page](../designations/complementary.qmd)
covers the GISC-Minor, Chemistry, Biology, and hydrology/water-resources
course bundles that students can layer on top of the FW degree.

**Asks**:

- Does the department agree with surfacing only **PAg + RPBio** as
  headline designation tracks?
- The complementary-pathways page suggests three curated electives
  bundles, water quality + biogeochemistry, aquatic ecology, and
  hydrology + water resources. Are there additional bundles the
  department would surface, or wording on these we want to refine?

# What is on the site

A quick orientation so you can review the most material content first.

| Page | What it is | Status |
|---|---|---|
| [Home](../index.qmd) | Landing page with land acknowledgement + Academic Calendar disclaimer + status table | Live |
| [Courses](../courses/index.qmd) | Filterable catalogue of 626 UBCO courses across 12 subjects | **Live data**, scraped daily from the UBC Okanagan Academic Calendar |
| [Designations → PAg](../designations/pag.qmd) | 150 BCIA-approved UBCO courses across 3 categories + 4-year sample plan | **Live data**, rendered from BCIA's June 2025 UBCO-specific list |
| [Designations → RPBio](../designations/rpbio.qmd) | CAB credentialing requirements mapped to UBCO courses with **gap warning** | Curated by program lead |
| [Designations → Complementary](../designations/complementary.qmd) | Chemistry, Biology, and water-resources elective bundles | Curated by program lead |
| [Outcomes](../outcomes/index.qmd) | v2 PLO list + I/R/M methodology + EESC 301 pilot pointer | Curated; curriculum-map matrix pending |
| [Skills](../skills/index.qmd) | Draft skills vocabulary (R, GIS, field methods, communication, etc.) | In development |
| [Planner](../planner/index.qmd) | Interactive degree planner (drag-drop + gap analysis) | In development |
| [Methodology](../docs/methodology.qmd) | Architecture, data feeds, scrape policy, refresh cadence | Live |

# How to give feedback

- **Email**: [nelson.jatel@ubc.ca](mailto:nelson.jatel@ubc.ca?subject=UBCO%20Freshwater%20Science%20-%20Department%20review)
  for direct comments, or to schedule a 30-minute walk-through.
- **GitHub issues**: open at the
  [project source repository](https://github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program/issues)
  if you prefer a tracked, threaded discussion.
- **Annotated PDF**: the entire site is also rendered as a printable
  PDF via Quarto if you would prefer to mark up a physical document
 , let me know and I'll send.

# Maintainer

**Dr. Nelson Jatel, PAg**
Lecturer and Freshwater Science Program Advisor
Department of Earth, Environmental and Geographic Sciences
UBC Okanagan
[nelson.jatel@ubc.ca](mailto:nelson.jatel@ubc.ca)

Site source code is open and at
[github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program](https://github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program).
